subject: Language Arts
sample_standards: Your child can tell how a character deals with a challenge or problem in a story.
Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.
Your child can use clues from pictures or objects to figure out what familiar words mean.
Your child can find words or phrases in a story that show what the narrator or speaker is thinking or feeling.
Your child can find details in a story that relate to the theme or main idea.
Your child shows understanding while reading or listening to stories, plays, or poems.
Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in print or online, to find how to say a word, what it means, or its part of speech.
Your child writes a conclusion that supports the information or explanation they shared.
canonical_name: Grade 6 Language Arts: Reading Comprehension — Literary Elements and Word Meaning
coherence_reason: Standards address both literary analysis (character, theme, narrator) and language/word meaning (using reference materials, context clues, grammar), which are related but distinct sub-concepts.

subject: Language Arts
sample_standards: Your child can name two things that have something in common, like both being big or both being quiet.
Your child uses tools to identify, describe, and draw special four-sided shapes and sorts flat shapes based on their features.
Your child can tell the difference between a physical map that shows land and water and a political map that shows countries and borders.
Your child can observe and describe shapes by their important features, sort and draw them, and begin learning about fractions by dividing shapes.
Your child uses tools to explore the features of triangles and circles.
Your child understands how Gestalt ideas help us see and understand things as whole patterns.
Your child learns about shapes by looking at their parts and how they fit together, and practices dividing shapes to understand fractions.
Your child can put together flat or solid shapes to make bigger shapes and then create new shapes from those.
canonical_name: Grade 6 Math: Geometry — Shapes and Their Properties
coherence_reason: Most standards are about geometry and shape properties, but two are about language arts (finding similarities) and geography (maps), and one references Gestalt psychology, causing poor topical coherence.
